content.text:
Memory stage: NoteConstructor v1.
        Current time: <instant>
        Timezone: Asia/Jerusalem
        Namespace: project:<0>

        Router decision: NOTE_WRITE
        Router memory types: NOTE, SOURCE, ENTITY
        Router reason: Document ingest uses deterministic note/source/entity route; hard claims/actionItems can be produced by later consolidation.

        Stage instructions:
        You are NoteConstructor v1 for a long-term AI agent.

Goal:
Construct reusable semantic notes from TARGET_MESSAGE when the material is contextual rather than a clean atomic fact.

A note is for decisions, rationale, design direction, plans, hypotheses, lessons, trade-offs, troubleshooting procedures, and compact document/context digests.
A note must add reusable meaning. It must not be a generic summary of the target turn.

Return JSON:
{
  "notes": [
    {
      "title": "short English title",
      "summary": "self-contained English semantic fragment",
      "note_type": "decision | direction | hypothesis | plan | lesson | doc_digest | context",
      "scope_json": {
        "kind": "global | conversation | entity | environment | document | project",
        "text": "human-readable applicability boundary",
        "basis": "explicit | inferred | summarized | imported"
      },
      "entity_refs": [
        {"entity_id": "resolved-entity-id", "role": "primary | secondary | mentioned | owner | subject"}
      ],
      "keywords": ["short search keyword"],
      "tags": ["short_tag"],
      "candidate_claim_hints": [],
      "confidence": 0.0,
      "importance": 1,
      "valid_from": "ISO-8601 or null",
      "valid_to": "ISO-8601 or null",
      "evidence_quote": "exact substring from TARGET_MESSAGE",
      "evidence_kind": "direct | summarized | imported | inferred | derived_from_note",
      "evidence_reason": "why this source supports the note",
      "rationale": "why this should be a note, not a claim"
    }
  ]
}

Rules:
- Write title, summary, keywords, tags, evidence_reason, and rationale in English.
- Preserve proper names, product names, repo names, file names, exact commands, exact identifiers, dates, and quoted terms.
- Use only entity IDs listed in Resolved entities.
- Never write raw labels such as "user", "assistant", "project", or "document" into entity_refs. If a note is about the user, use the resolved USER entity id from Resolved entities.
- For project-level notes without a more specific subject, use the resolved namespace/project subject entity.
- Use TARGET_MESSAGE as the only evidence. Earlier messages are context only.
- evidence_quote must be an exact short substring copied from TARGET_MESSAGE source data.
- Do not construct notes from greetings, phatic chatter, pure questions, one-off commands with no durable rationale, or memory mechanics discussion that contains no reusable project/user decision.
- Do not construct notes from a single weak uncertain factual observation when source recall is enough and there is no rationale, decision, plan, lesson, or reusable analysis.
- Do not duplicate a claim candidate as a note when a precise claim is enough.
- Do use a note when the target captures "why", "how", "we decided", "current approach", trade-offs, working agreements, troubleshooting order, or a design direction.
- If TARGET_MESSAGE is a document or document section, preserve document title/source/section names and create section-scoped notes for important definitions, component roles, workflows, policies, and lists.
- For dense technical documents and prompt packs, prefer several strong section-scoped notes over one broad document summary. Keep only reusable notes; do not summarize every paragraph.
- For one document section, return at most two notes. If a section defines one compact component or policy, return one note.
- If source metadata force_memory_write=true, create a note when the content is reusable context but not a clean claim/action item/profile update.
- For named components, include the exact component names in title, summary, keywords, or tags so later retrieval can find the component role.
- A troubleshooting/procedure note is allowed, but do not create an action item unless the target creates a commitment.
- Keep each note self-contained: future recall should understand it without the original conversation.
- Keep summary concise: usually one or two sentences.
- Prefer zero notes over broad summary spam.
- Return valid JSON only.

        Relevant persisted memory for dedup/context only:
        - note hot-path:note:<0> DOC_DIGEST; Five-tier memory model separates prompt context, typed memory, notes, evidence, and indexes; The architecture defines five storage tiers: Tier A for minimal in-context prompt blocks, Tier B for typed long-term records (`Entity`, `Claim`, `Profile`, `Task`, `Episode`, `Source`, `MemoryRun`), Tier C for semantic note structures (`Note`, `NoteSource`, `NoteEntity`, `NoteLink`), Tier D for immutable raw evidence, and Tier E for retrieval indexes over stored data.; scope=Applies to section '01. Architecture / 5. Storage tiers .. 6. Structured scope' in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md
- note hot-path:note:<1> DOC_DIGEST; Recommended architecture pipeline and component roles; The recommended flow routes source inputs through Memory Router and Write-Time Retrieval into Entity Canonicalizer, then branches to Claim/Task/Profile/Note processing with reconciliers writing to Postgres + pgvector; background workers perform consolidation, repair, and retention, while runtime answering uses Read-Time Retrieval Planner and Answer Composer.; scope=Applies to section '01. Architecture / 3. Основная модель записи .. 4. Рекомендуемая архитектура' in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md
- note hot-path:note:<2> DIRECTION; Read path uses mode selection plus v3 budget caps and cautious answer composition; The read path requires deciding whether memory is needed, loading core blocks, selecting retrieval mode (Claim-first, Note-first, Mixed, Evidence-first/fallback), enforcing explicit v3 per-mode retrieval caps, and composing answers that track verification status, conflicts, scope, abstention, and note-vs-fact distinction.; scope=Applies to section '01. Architecture / 11. Write path .. 12. Read path' in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md
- note hot-path:note:<3> DIRECTION; Write path is an 8-step transactional pipeline with retrieval-before-extraction; The write path is a strict 8-step flow: persist source, route (`noop`/`direct_structured_write`/`note_write`/`mixed`), retrieve candidate memories before extraction, canonicalize entities, extract typed candidates, reconcile with existing items, apply all mutations in one transaction, then schedule maintenance jobs.; scope=Applies to section '01. Architecture / 11. Write path .. 12. Read path' in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md
- note hot-path:note:<4> DOC_DIGEST; Responsibility split across runtime, memory worker, and storage layer; `User-facing runtime` handles dialogue, tool orchestration, read-time retrieval, and cheap routing/escalation, and must avoid heavy reconciliation/repair. `Memory worker / manager` owns routing, retrieval-before-update, canonicalization, extraction, reconciliation, consolidation, repair, and retention. `Storage layer` is responsible for immutable evidence, typed tables, transactional updates, lineage, indexes, and audit trail.; scope=Applies to section '01. Architecture / 10. Разделение ответственности' in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md
- note hot-path:note:<5> DOC_DIGEST; Two semantic write paths and two maintenance paths define memory flow; The architecture explicitly separates memory handling into two semantic write paths (Direct structured path and Note path) and two maintenance paths (Consolidation and Repair), establishing when to store hard structured data versus contextual notes and how quality is maintained over time.; scope=Applies to section '01. Architecture / 3. Основная модель записи .. 4. Рекомендуемая архитектура' in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md

        Resolved entities:
        - mention=Postgres; action=CREATE_NEW; id=entity:<0>; type=TECHNOLOGY; name=Postgres; reason=Concrete technology explicitly listed as sufficient for memory layer.
- mention=JSONB; action=CREATE_NEW; id=entity:<1>; type=TECHNOLOGY; name=JSONB; reason=Concrete technical capability named in the section.
- mention=pgvector; action=CREATE_NEW; id=entity:<2>; type=TECHNOLOGY; name=pgvector; reason=Named technology component in the architecture guidance.
- mention=full-text indexes; action=CREATE_NEW; id=entity:<3>; type=TECHNOLOGY; name=full-text indexes; reason=Reusable technical concept explicitly listed.
- mention=lineage tables; action=CREATE_NEW; id=entity:<4>; type=CONCEPT; name=lineage tables; reason=Named architectural data-model concept in the list.
- mention=Graph DB; action=CREATE_NEW; id=entity:<5>; type=TECHNOLOGY; name=Graph DB; reason=Explicitly referenced as a distinct technology option.
- mention=current project; action=CREATE_NEW; id=entity:<6>; type=PROJECT; name=Current project; reason=Default current-project subject for claims without a more specific resolved subject

        Retrieval plan context:
        entity_queries=01. Architecture|agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md|01. Architecture / 17. Почему не надо начинать с graph DB|MARKDOWN
        text_queries=01. Architecture agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md 01. Architecture / 17. Почему не надо начинать с graph DB MARKDOWN Document title: 01. Architecture Document source: agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md Document section: 01. Architecture / 17. Почему не надо начинать с graph DB Document lines: 547-558 ## 17. Почему не надо начинать с graph DB `Entity + Claim + NoteLink + scope_json` уже дают graph-like semantics. Для memory layer обычно хватает: - Postgres - JSONB - pgvector - full-text indexes - lineage tables Graph DB нужен, когда traversal/path queries становятся частью продукта, а не просто внутренним удобством.
        predicate_hints=

        TARGET_MESSAGE source data:
        [external:document-section:9ab04f0af9d4842289e56dca233345ed] EXTERNAL
Source metadata: source_kind=document; origin=pasted_document_section; document_type=MARKDOWN; source_ref=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md; title=01. Architecture; heading=01. Architecture / 17. Почему не надо начинать с graph DB; imported_at=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z
Document title: 01. Architecture
Document source: agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md
Document section: 01. Architecture / 17. Почему не надо начинать с graph DB
Document lines: 547-558

## 17. Почему не надо начинать с graph DB

`Entity + Claim + NoteLink + scope_json` уже дают graph-like semantics.
Для memory layer обычно хватает:

- Postgres
- JSONB
- pgvector
- full-text indexes
- lineage tables

Graph DB нужен, когда traversal/path queries становятся частью продукта, а не просто внутренним удобством.

responseFormat.description: Construct contextual memory note candidates from one target message.
responseFormat.name: memory_note_constructor

systemPrompts:
You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.
